question: Will Mara Voss's scheduling copilot reach a committed public launch or at least three signed design partners by mid-September 2025?

board_vote_record.description: auto-declared: institution 'seed_investor_board' members write this decision record; minimal shape the aggregation reads

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

seed_investor_board.evidence: Priya Shah chairs 3-seat board; majority rule assumed for standard governance

launch_spend_constraint.evidence: Mara holds 40k discretionary budget; board must approve spend over 50k

pilot_expiration.rule: pilot_expansion_decision must occur within 3 weeks of 2025-07-18

board_decision_visibility.rule: board_vote_record is visible to all board members and CEO after meeting

pilot_decision_visibility.rule: pilot_expansion_decision is visible to Mara Voss and Devon Reyes immediately

mara_voss.why_consequential: controls discretionary budget up to 40k; can initiate launch commitment within that budget; drives design partner acquisition

devon_reyes.why_consequential: decides whether Calder Logistics pilot expands, which could become a design partner

priya_shah.why_consequential: controls board agenda and vote; must approve any launch spend exceeding 50k

unresolved_mechanisms: identity of the other two board members (member_2, member_3) is unknown
whether Calder Logistics pilot expansion would convert to a design partner agreement is unspecified
whether Mara Voss can secure additional design partners beyond Calder Logistics is unknown
assumptions: board operates on simple majority rule (2 of 3 votes)
pilot expansion decision will occur within 3 weeks from prediction date
design partner agreements require signed contracts, not verbal commitments
public launch commitment requires either board approval (if >50k spend) or Mara's discretionary authority (if <=40k)

issues_first_pass: process 'board_approval_process' needs at least two states
process 'pilot_expansion_process' needs at least two states
auto_repairs: institution 'seed_investor_board' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No entity or fact type for 'signed design partner' as a distinct contractual milestone separate from 'design_partner_agreement' status field — the model conflates signing with agreement creation, but a signed agreement may still be contingent on board approval or payment terms
No representation of calendar time constraints (e.g., mid-September 2025 deadline) as a resource that can be exhausted or trigger urgency — time is only a field, not a process driver
No entity for 'public launch' as a distinct event with prerequisites (e.g., feature completeness, marketing readiness, legal clearance) — only a boolean commitment flag
No mechanism for counting design partners across multiple agreements or tracking whether they are distinct companies vs. same company multiple agreements
relabeled_progress_bars: 'design_partner_acquisition_process' states are labeled as linear (prospecting→negotiating→signed) but real-world acquisition often loops back from negotiating to prospecting due to renegotiation or new terms — this is a progress bar, not a state machine
'pilot_expansion_process' states imply expansion is binary (expanded/not_expanded) but real pilots often partially expand (e.g., one department) — the model treats it as a binary progress bar
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes board approval is only needed for launch spend >50k, but Mara Voss's discretionary budget is 40k — if launch costs exceed 40k, she must seek board approval, but the model does not model cost estimation or budget overrun risk

The model treats 'design_partner_signing' as a single event, but real design partners may require internal legal review, which can fail silently — no 'lost' state after 'negotiating' is modeled as a possible outcome of signing attempt
The model does not account for the possibility that a design partner signing could be rescinded or contingent on product milestones — the 'signed' state is terminal
No representation of competitor actions or market timing that could force early launch or delay
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Mara Voss's affordance 'spend_up_to_40000_on_launch' encodes a human budget limit but does not encode her risk tolerance or willingness to spend that full amount — she may hoard budget
Devon Reyes's affordances include 'delay_decision' but no process state for 'delayed' in pilot_expansion_process — the model assumes a decision is made, but humans often defer indefinitely
Priya Shah's affordance 'influence_other_board_members' is listed but no mechanism for influence (e.g., persuasion, voting blocs) — it's a label, not a model
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The 'public_launch_committed' fact type conflates a public announcement with an internal commitment — a company may internally commit but not announce publicly, or announce without internal readiness

The 'design_partner_count' fact type treats signed agreements as public knowledge, but companies often keep design partners confidential until launch — the model cannot distinguish between signed and publicly announced
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of software development lifecycle (e.g., feature freeze, beta testing, bug fixes) that could delay launch regardless of business commitments
No representation of regulatory or compliance checks (e.g., data privacy, accessibility) that could block public launch
No representation of infrastructure dependencies (e.g., cloud service uptime, API availability) that could affect pilot expansion or launch
No representation of financial runway or cash burn rate — the model has a budget but no timeline for when money runs out
